classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

title: Privations in Things are Fittingly Ordered by God.

source.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

verses.book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: Yet even these privations of things are so ordered in the universe of nature, that to those wisely considering they not unfittingly have their vicissitudes.

verses.book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: For by not illuminating certain places and times, God has also made the darkness as fittingly as the day.

verses.book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: For if we by restraining the voice fittingly interpose silence in speaking, how much more does He, as the perfect framer of all things, fittingly make privations of things?

verses.book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: Whence also in the hymn of the three children, light and darkness alike praise God, that is, bring forth praise in the hearts of those who well consider.
